prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "平板转体" (plank with rotation) exercise, front-three-quarter view. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a plank rotation: starting from a high plank with arms straight; currently rotated to the left so that the right hand is on the ground (supporting), the left arm is extended straight up toward the ceiling, and the body has rotated 90 degrees so the body is now in a side-plank-like position; the legs are in a stacked position; head following the rotation; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: obliques (rotational core) and rectus abdominis. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: shoulder stabilizers and gluteus medius. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques (rotational core); "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "肩稳定肌 / 臀中肌" pointing to the shoulder and gluteus medius stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
